chapters.title: Common myths let men off the hook for bad behavior
chapters.text:
In any partnership, your partner's mindset shapes the relationship, and many men fall short. Worse, they invent reasons for treating women poorly, and other men copy those reasons. These so-called reasons are myths, and this chapter breaks them apart.

There are plenty of excuses and very little effort to change.

The first myth says a man will act right for the right woman. This claim is almost always false. It assumes that bad behavior is a shield he uses when he feels scared. A man who treats you well chooses to grow on his own and chooses to grow for you as well.

Real change starts with a deliberate choice to do better.

A second myth claims that every man cheats, and a faithful man is rare. This is another story used to excuse harm. Cheating is not something people are born to do. No one is wired to be unfaithful. When a man loves you, he stays true. Modern culture has pushed this story so hard that many people accept it. The belief that cheating happens even in loving partnerships is wrong and damaging.

Many women stay in unhappy partnerships because they accept this story. They take infidelity and disrespect as part of the deal. That is not the way love works. A serious commitment should be free of disloyalty.

Loyalty is a daily choice, not a switch you can flip on later.

The last myth is the worst. It says men love confident women. That sounds nice, but it is rarely true. Many men feel threatened by confidence and work hard to break it down. When a man meets a confident woman, his ego is hurt, and he sees her as a challenge to his sense of power.

So he spends his time trying to shrink her. Once she is under his thumb, he feels in control. Confident women often end up single or with men who chip away at their confidence through gaslighting and sly insults.

This summary will help you see why self-respect comes first. You will also learn to spot the excuses sleazy men use, so you can ignore them and stop chasing a man who does not deserve you.

Many women live with unfaithful or abusive partners and stay out of fear of stigma or because they share children with them.

chapters.title: Knowing when to walk away can save your life
chapters.text:
Many women hesitate to leave partnerships that have become unhappy, or that were unhappy from the start. There is a social cost when a woman leaves, especially a marriage, because people see it as her failure to keep a man. Society has trapped women in situations that hurt them, or worse, put their lives at risk.

Society has left women with few choices about how they should be treated.

Learning to walk away is a skill that needs practice and readiness. Men who know a woman will be judged for leaving often make things worse. The result is more harm, both verbal and physical.

When you accept bad treatment, you open the door to worse.

When a man treats you poorly, it is not because he does not know better. He knows there are no consequences. Let him get away with it once, and he will try again. Forgive him when he hurts you, and he will test how much more you can take. If he wants to push the limit, he will not stop until you stop him.

No one else will guard your happiness and safety. Take that step today.

When there is abuse or mistreatment in a relationship, you have to speak up about his actions. If he keeps going, find the door and use it. Your happiness is not up for debate. You have to speak for yourself. Staying quiet is not an option, because your happiness belongs to you.

A man who shapes up only for the right woman is a false idea, and a harmful one to keep spreading, as Derrick Jaxn has written.

chapters.title: The relationship and sex dynamic takes work
chapters.text:
Sex plays a big role in modern relationships. It gets hyped and treated like a prize. Some people enter relationships just to have sex, and some have sex just to feel something. People put on masks, lie, and scheme to get what they want. That kind of behavior cheapens the place of real intimacy in a relationship.

Putting sex on a pedestal can make you desperate for it.

This pressure has given rise to many strategies for the early stages of dating. Some women hold back sex during the talking stage to test a man. This tactic can work, but some men are patient, and that patience is not always a good sign. Once he knows you are playing this game, he will simply wait you out.

Healthy partnerships grow through give and take.

Some men wear a good-guy mask long enough to get what they want, then disappear. Watch out for the self-styled good guy. He is a narcissist who believes sex is owed to him. A good man wants to know you first and to share himself with you before sex happens, on both sides. If he has to trick you to get in your bed, you should be careful. If you sense he is only after sex, trust your gut and walk away.

In a new relationship, sex should grow out of shared interest and shared intention.

Do not assume a man is good just because he was charming on the first date. Pay close attention to how he acts, speaks, and thinks at other times, not only when you are dressed up for dinner.

chapters.title: Common mistakes to skip in new relationships
chapters.text:
Women make a lot of early mistakes in dating, and those mistakes can lead to big problems later. The cause is often past heartbreak or a fear of being alone. Men will pick up on that fear nine times out of ten if you let them.

Never let your need for a partner show on the first few dates.

First, do not assume he is a nice guy on early dates. If he keeps saying he is, watch out. He is trying to convince you and himself. Time will show who he really is, so pay attention and you will see the changes.

Do not judge a person until you have watched them for a long stretch.

Second, do not put up with even a hint of bad behavior. If you let it slide, it will grow. If he is rude to the waiter or the valet, he will be worse to you. Small things you ignore often turn into bigger problems. Cut off bad behavior the moment you see it.

Do not make excuses for bad behavior. Address it right away.

Third, never make excuses for his flaws. If he said or did something wrong, do not justify it. He is probably watching to see what he can get away with. Remember your worth and decide how you want to be treated.

chapters.title: A wedding will not fix a broken relationship
chapters.text:
Marriage can be a beautiful thing, the natural next step after months or years of steady love. It should happen because two people in love decide to build a life together.

Marriage works best after a steady and happy partnership.

It should not come from pressure or panic. You both need a clear picture of what you want. Some people think marriage is a quick fix for a struggling relationship, and they jump in and regret it later.

A long-term promise to a bad partner will not change who they are.

When a relationship starts to fail, some people want to lock it down with a wedding. That only deepens the crisis. People say marriage will make him treat you better or finally step up. That is not true. Marriage can make things worse.

A bad relationship leads to a bad marriage. Do not walk into a bad situation out of fear.

A wedding gives a man a sense of control. If he has been mistreating you, marriage only confirms his habits. Marriage also makes it harder to leave, because ending a marriage means a messy divorce, while dating can just stop.

chapters.title: Loyalty is the foundation of every healthy relationship
chapters.text:
In any partnership, trust and loyalty matter more than anything. Do not accept the lie that loyalty is hard or even impossible for men. It is not, and the lie is just an excuse for cheating. A healthy relationship rests on fidelity.

Loyalty is not rare. Demand it without apology from any partner.

When a relationship lacks loyalty, many things fall apart. A cheating partner chips away at the other person self-esteem and makes her question her own worth. That kind of damage can spread to her work and her life.

Without loyalty, there is room for chaos and pain.

Without loyalty, there is no trust. Without trust, there is no love. A relationship full of betrayal becomes empty, exhausting, and harmful. Nothing real can grow in that kind of soil. Stay away from a chronic cheater, and never put up with betrayal when it shows up.

If you spot bad behavior early on, speak up and push for change. If he cannot or will not change, do not commit to him. You deserve a partner who takes you seriously and puts your well-being above his impulses. If he cannot drop a bad habit for you, he is the wrong person.

A relationship without loyalty has no love in it, and it will only turn toxic.

When you start a new relationship, or when you are already in one, make sure loyalty sits at the center of it. Without loyalty, there is no love, and without love, there is only hurt and harm. Do not accept that. Remember, your worth is not up for grabs.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Dating has become a battlefield for women. They have to come up with clever plans to win. The prize is a real relationship. Men, on the other hand, can do very little and still be seen as the prize that women must chase. That setup means a man can act any way he wants and still find a partner, while women have to clear hurdle after hurdle. This has to change. Women have to rediscover their worth and take back their power.

They have to ask for what they want without shame. They need honest, open talks about how they expect to be treated, both before and during the relationship. They need partners who take the lead in doing better, not partners who shrug it off. To take back their power, women have to watch out for men who try to chip away at their confidence or crush their self-esteem.

Some men hide behind a good-guy mask and are not good at all. Women need to reject the idea that cheating is a right reserved for the right woman, and they need to demand loyalty from their partners. Remember, a wedding will not fix a bad relationship. If a partner will not change, a ring will not work magic on him.

Telling someone to leave the past behind is a sign that he does not want to hear about the painful experiences that shaped how she moves through relationships. It is the language of weak men, as Derrick Jaxn has written.

If the man you are with makes you doubt yourself, picks at you, and gaslights you, you are with a narcissist, and you need to leave now.
